title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe | Easy Chinese Soup

seo.h1: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe: Spicy, Tangy & Delicious
h2Headings: What is Hot and Sour Soup?
Ingredients You Need for Hot and Sour Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup, garnished with green onions and chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with traditional Chinese flavors.

description: This classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup is a symphony of flavors, combining savory broth with a delightful kick of spice and a tangy finish. It's a comforting and invigorating dish, perfect for any occasion and surprisingly easy to make at home. Enjoy the rich textures and aromatic spices in every spoonful.

ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes
mixed with 3 tbsp cold water to make a slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Slice the pork loin into thin matchsticks, dice the tofu, and slice the rehydrated shiitake and wood ear mushrooms. Prepare the cornstarch slurry by mixing cornstarch with cold water. Beat the eggs in a small bowl and set aside.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Solids
instructions.text: In a large pot, bring the chicken or vegetable broth to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Add the sliced pork, tofu, shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Cook for 5-7 minutes, until the pork is cooked through.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the rice vinegar, soy sauce, and white pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more vinegar for tang or white pepper for heat. The soup should have a balanced hot and sour flavor.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir and slowly pour it into the simmering soup while continuously stirring. Continue to stir until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. This usually takes about 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Egg Ribbons
instructions.text: Once the soup is thickened, slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the hot soup in a thin stream, stirring gently in one direction with a spoon or ladle. This will create delicate egg ribbons. Cook for about 1 minute until the egg is set.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the toasted sesame oil. Ladle the hot and sour soup into individual bowls. Garnish each serving with fresh sliced green onions and a drizzle of chili oil, if desired. Serve immediately and enjoy the authentic flavors.

tips: For authentic flavor, do not skimp on the white pepper and rice vinegar; they are key to the soup's characteristic 'hot' and 'sour' profile.
The cornstarch slurry must be stirred thoroughly before adding to the soup, and added slowly while stirring constantly to prevent lumps and ensure even thickening.
Adjust the level of spiciness and sourness to your personal preference. Add more chili oil or vinegar at the table for a customizable experience.
article:
Hot and Sour Soup is more than just a dish; it's an experience. A cornerstone of Chinese cuisine, particularly beloved in Sichuan and Hunan provinces, this soup delivers a thrilling dance of flavors and textures that awaken the palate. Its name perfectly encapsulates its essence: a fiery kick from chili and white pepper, balanced by the tangy zing of vinegar, all swirled into a savory, umami-rich broth. For many, it's the quintessential start to a Chinese meal, offering comfort and excitement in every spoonful.

If you've ever savored the complex layers of an authentic bowl, you know its magic. But imagine bringing that restaurant-quality flavor right into your own kitchen. This article will guide you through the journey of creating this iconic soup, providing a detailed Hot and Sour Soup Recipe that's surprisingly straightforward. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your guests with a homemade version that rivals the best takeout.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

At its core, Hot and Sour Soup is a Chinese culinary masterpiece known for its distinct flavor profile. Originating from regions celebrated for their bold and spicy cooking, it beautifully marries two powerful tastes: "hot" from ground white pepper and often chili oil, and "sour" from Chinese black vinegar or rice vinegar. Beyond these primary flavors, the soup is deeply savory, thanks to a rich broth, soy sauce, and a medley of earthy mushrooms.

The texture is equally important. Unlike clear broths, Hot and Sour Soup is typically thickened to a silky consistency with cornstarch, allowing it to coat the spoon and warm you from the inside out. It's also visually appealing, often featuring delicate strands of egg swirled into the broth, alongside a colorful assortment of shredded pork, bamboo shoots, and various mushrooms. It’s a dish that embodies balance – heat meeting tang, savory meeting earthy, and tender ingredients swimming in a satisfyingly thick broth.

## Ingredients You Need for Hot and Sour Soup

Crafting an authentic Hot and Sour Soup requires a few key components, many of which are readily available in Asian markets or even well-stocked grocery stores. The quality of your ingredients directly impacts the final flavor, so opt for fresh where possible.

**For the Broth Base:**
*   6 cups chicken broth (low sodium preferred) or vegetable broth for a vegetarian version.

**Proteins and Vegetables:**
*   4 ounces lean pork, shredded into thin strips (pork loin or tenderloin works well). For vegetarian, omit pork and increase tofu.
*   8 ounces firm or silken tofu, cut into small matchsticks or cubes.
*   6-8 dried shiitake mushrooms, rehydrated and thinly sliced (reserve mushroom soaking liquid).
*   1 ounce dried wood ear mushrooms (or cloud ear fungus), rehydrated and thinly sliced.
*   1 (8-ounce) can bamboo shoots, drained and julienned.
*   1 large egg, beaten.
*   2-3 tablespoons fresh green onions, chopped, for garnish.

**Flavor Enhancers & Seasonings:**
*   3-4 tablespoons Chinese black vinegar (Chinkiang vinegar is ideal) or rice vinegar.
*   2-3 tablespoons light soy sauce.
*   1-2 tablespoons dark soy sauce (for color and deeper flavor).
*   1-2 teaspoons ground white pepper (this is crucial for the "hot" element).
*   1-2 tablespoons chili oil (optional, for extra heat).
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil.
*   1-2 tablespoons cornstarch, dissolved in 3-4 tablespoons cold water (cornstarch slurry).

**Optional but Recommended:**
*   A small piece of fresh ginger, grated (for aromatics).
*   Dried lily flowers (golden needles), rehydrated (adds a unique texture and flavor).

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Hot and Sour Soup

Making Hot and Sour Soup from scratch is a rewarding experience, and this Hot and Sour Soup Recipe breaks it down into easy, manageable steps.

1.  **Prepare Your Ingredients:** Start by rehydrating your dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in separate bowls of hot water for at least 20-30 minutes. Once soft, squeeze out excess water, remove the tough stems from shiitake, and thinly slice both types. Reserve about 1/2 cup of the shiitake soaking liquid for extra umami in your broth. Shred your pork, slice your tofu and bamboo shoots, and beat your egg. Prepare your cornstarch slurry.

2.  **Build the Broth:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, bring the chicken or vegetable broth to a simmer over medium-high heat. Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and the reserved mushroom soaking liquid (strained, to remove any grit). If using ginger, add it now. Bring back to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.

3.  **Add Proteins and Seasonings:** Add the shredded pork to the simmering broth, stirring gently until it’s cooked through, about 2-3 minutes. Next, add the tofu. Season the soup with light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and a generous amount of white pepper. This is where the "hot" really starts to build.

4.  **Introduce the Sourness:** Stir in the Chinese black vinegar. Start with 3 tablespoons and taste. You can always add more later to achieve your desired level of sourness. At this stage, it's vital to taste and adjust the balance of flavors – ensuring the hot, sour, and savory elements are harmonious.

5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give your cornstarch slurry a quick stir to ensure the cornstarch hasn't settled. Slowly pour the slurry into the simmering soup in a thin stream, stirring constantly, until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. You might not need all of it. The soup should be velvety, not overly gloopy. Following this Hot and Sour Soup Recipe carefully will ensure the perfect texture.

6.  **Add the Egg Flower:** Once the soup is thickened, increase the heat slightly to maintain a gentle simmer. Slowly pour the beaten egg into the soup in a thin, steady stream while gently stirring the soup with a spoon or ladle in a circular motion. This creates beautiful, delicate strands of "egg flower."

7.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the sesame oil and chili oil (if using). Ladle into bowls and garnish with chopped green onions. Your homemade Hot and Sour Soup is ready to be enjoyed!

## Pro Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup Results

Achieving that perfect balance of flavors and textures in your Hot and Sour Soup can be elevated with a few expert tricks.

*   **Don't Skimp on White Pepper:** White pepper is a signature component of the "hot" in this soup, providing a distinct, aromatic heat different from chili. Don't be shy with it; it truly makes a difference.
*   **Taste and Adjust Constantly:** The beauty of homemade soup is customizing it. As you add vinegar, soy sauce, and white pepper, taste the soup. Everyone's preference for hot, sour, and savory varies. Adjust until it's perfect for you. This Hot and Sour Soup Recipe is a guide, but your palate is the ultimate judge.
*   **Cornstarch Slurry Control:** Always add the cornstarch slurry gradually. You can always add more, but you can't easily remove it. The soup should be thick enough to cling to a spoon but still pourable.
*   **The Perfect Egg Flower:** For delicate, wispy egg strands, ensure your soup is at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil. Pour the egg in a very thin stream while gently stirring the soup in one direction. Stop stirring once all the egg is in to allow it to set.
*   **Use Quality Broth:** A good quality chicken or vegetable broth forms the foundation of your soup's flavor. If possible, use homemade broth for an even richer taste.

## Serving Suggestions for Hot and Sour Soup

Hot and Sour Soup is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways.

*   **As an Appetizer:** This is its most common role in Chinese restaurants. It's a fantastic way to kick off a meal, stimulating the appetite with its vibrant flavors.
*   **With Other Chinese Dishes:** Pair it with classic mains like General Tso's chicken, Kung Pao shrimp, steamed fish, or a simple vegetable stir-fry. Its robust flavor profile complements a wide range of dishes.
*   **Alongside Rice or Noodles:** A bowl of Hot and Sour Soup with a side of steamed white rice or a small portion of plain noodles makes for a comforting and satisfying light meal on its own.
*   **Garnish Generously:** Don't forget the fresh garnishes! Chopped green onions are a must. A sprinkle of fresh cilantro, an extra drizzle of chili oil for those who love heat, or a dash more black vinegar can elevate each individual serving.

## Variations and Substitutions for Hot and Sour Soup

One of the great things about this soup is its adaptability. This versatile Hot and Sour Soup Recipe allows for many adaptations to suit dietary needs or personal preferences.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make it vegetarian by using vegetable broth instead of chicken broth and omitting the pork. For a vegan version, ensure your broth is vegan and check that your soy sauces are free of animal products. Increase the amount of tofu and mushrooms for added substance.
*   **Different Proteins:** If pork isn't your preference, thinly sliced chicken breast or shrimp can be used. Add shrimp towards the end of cooking as they cook very quickly.
*   **Mushroom Medley:** Experiment with different types of fresh or dried mushrooms. Enoki mushrooms, oyster mushrooms, or even regular button mushrooms can add depth and texture.
*   **Adjusting Heat and Sourness:** The beauty of making it at home is customization. Increase or decrease the amount of white pepper and chili oil for your desired level of "hot." Similarly, adjust the black vinegar to achieve your perfect "sour."
*   **Gluten-Free:** Substitute regular soy sauce with tamari to make this Hot and Sour Soup Recipe gluten-free. Ensure your broth and other seasonings are also gluten-free.

Bringing the authentic flavors of Chinese Hot and Sour Soup into your home kitchen is a truly rewarding culinary adventure. With this comprehensive Hot and Sour Soup Recipe, you have all the tools and knowledge to create a bowl that’s bursting with the perfect balance of heat, tang, and savory depth. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight in one of the most beloved Chinese soups. Happy cooking!
